title: The "I'll lose it" voice: whose is it?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for tracing the fear-of-loss belief back to its source.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Week 6 of the MONEY program. Five prompts for locating where the conviction that money will leave came from, what it protects against, and what replacing it with trust might open.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Week 6 daily reflections in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you notice you can't enjoy a good financial period because part of you is already preparing for the loss.

content.text: A journal prompt set from Week 6 of the MONEY program. The fear of losing money is often a borrowed fear: someone else's voice, someone else's experience. These prompts trace it.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
What specific fears come up when I think about losing money?
Write them out plainly. Name the scenario: losing a job, a crash, a failed investment, a surprise expense.
What do those fears reveal about my trust in myself or in life?
Write what the fear is protecting you from believing.
Who said "I'll lose it" first? Whose voice is the fear-of-loss voice?
Write the person, the moment, the context. How old were you when you heard it?
What experiences remind me that money, ideas, or opportunities return or renew?
Write the specific examples. Times money came back. Times a situation recovered. Evidence.
What would change in how I spend, invest, and hold money if I believed it stays?
Write the specific decision you'd make differently. One concrete thing.
Adapted from Week 6 daily reflections in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
